title: Mega Beedrill Battle Guide
summary: An extreme-speed [bug]/[poison] physical ace whose [adaptability] turns every STAB into a 2x boost, letting 150 Attack and 145 Speed tear through unboosted teams.

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Beedrill is an extreme-speed [bug]/[poison] physical ace whose identity is defined entirely by [adaptability]. The ability upgrades STAB from 1.5x to 2x, turning [u-turn] and [poison-jab] into metagame-level damage from just 150 Attack. Combined with 145 Speed, it outruns nearly every unboosted attacker and kills them before they can retaliate.

## Strengths
[adaptability] is a true damage multiplier: every STAB move effectively gains 33% damage on top of 150 Attack. 145 Speed sits above most scarfed threats without needing an item, freeing the item slot for [beedrillite]. [u-turn] keeps the pivot tempo advantage — every hit comes with a free matchup read — and [knock-off] still strips items, which means Mega Beedrill is a rare sweeper that also does chore work.

## Weaknesses & Answers
40 Defense and 80 SpD are unchanged, so a single priority hit or neutral physical attack can end the run. Rock, Fire, Flying, and psychic weaknesses all remain live, and Mega Beedrill loses the Speed race entirely if the opponent brings a choice-scarf user above 145. The pre-Mega turn runs swarm instead of [adaptability], so telegraphed weakness hits before evolution can cut the sweep short.

## Recommended Moveset
[u-turn] is locked as the main STAB because [adaptability] plus pivot utility is the whole point. [poison-jab] is the mandatory [poison] STAB finisher, also [adaptability]-boosted, that punishes Fairies and mirror Grass. [drill-run] is the Ground coverage for [steel], Rock, and Electric walls that would otherwise wall the combo outright. [knock-off] is the last slot for item removal and long-game resource generation.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[beedrillite] is mandatory — there is no alternative. [adaptability] is locked post-evolution. Tera [bug] maxes [u-turn] damage for a true one-shot slot, while Tera [steel] flips the Rock, Flying, and psychic weaknesses into a defensive patch. Tera Fighting and Tera Ground are rarer picks for specific team comps that need the coverage.

## Teammates & Synergy
Mega Beedrill is effectively mandatory paired with a hazard remover — defog or rapid-spin — because stealth-rock deletes 12% of its HP per entry. Fake-out users and priority blockers like a taunt lead help shield the pre-Mega turn, and a cleaner behind the pivot cycle capitalises on the chip. Pivot partners with volt-switch or flip-turn keep the [u-turn] loop spinning across the whole match.

## Play Patterns
Only enter the field when stealth-rock is gone. Mega Evolve into a predicted switch, then [u-turn] on every soft matchup and drop [poison-jab] or [drill-run] only when you have a guaranteed KO. Save Tera for a telegraphed Rock or Flying priority move.
